axis: nexus external-API bridge subsystem — header pass-through (UA + Accept) restoration

trigger.rationale: 8 of the 14 ω-bridge-3 migrations implicitly dropped polite-UA / Accept headers when switching to bridge_helper.sh. All 16 PASS empirically with default curl, but explicit polite identity is best-practice (some upstream APIs throttle anonymous UAs more aggressively over time). This cycle restores that hygiene without modifying the upstream call patterns themselves.

env_vars_added.name: BRIDGE_HELPER_UA
env_vars_added.semantics: if set, passed as `-A "$BRIDGE_HELPER_UA"` to curl (User-Agent override)
env_vars_added.default_when_unset: curl default UA (curl/<version>) — backward-compatible with the 16 existing migrations

env_vars_added.name: BRIDGE_HELPER_HEADERS
env_vars_added.semantics: if set, treated as multi-line newline-separated; each non-empty line passed as `-H "$line"` (Accept, Authorization, X-* etc.)

env_vars_added.name: BRIDGE_HELPER_TIMEOUT
env_vars_added.semantics: if set, passed as `--max-time "$BRIDGE_HELPER_TIMEOUT"` (per-call override of MAX_TIME)

helper_change_summary.implementation_pattern: single bash array `extra_args=()` assembled once after arg parsing; expanded via bash-3.2-safe `${extra_args[@]+"${extra_args[@]}"}` idiom (avoids unbound-variable trap from `set -u` on empty array under macOS-default bash 3.2.57). Applied uniformly to (a) DISABLE-mode raw passthrough and (b) the retry-loop curl call.
helper_change_summary.header_doc_block: added 6-line block to header comment listing the new env vars (lines 36-44 of new file), plus an ω-bridge-4 traceability paragraph pointing back to this JSON.
helper_change_summary.verbose_logging: BRIDGE_HELPER_VERBOSE=1 now logs `UA set:`, `header set:`, and `timeout override:` lines to stderr, allowing forensic verification that env vars are actually consumed.

bridges_updated.name: cmb
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-A 'Mozilla/5.0'` (browser-spoofed); replaced with polite identity for transparency. Wikipedia tolerates either — politeness preferred.

bridges_updated.name: nanograv
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-A 'Mozilla/5.0 nanograv-bridge'`; replaced with proper polite identity (no Mozilla spoof).

bridges_updated.name: icecube
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-A 'Mozilla/5.0 icecube-bridge'`; replaced with proper polite identity. NASA endpoint (gcn.gsfc.nasa.gov) particularly benefits from polite identity per common-sense API hygiene.

bridges_updated.name: arxiv
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-A 'nexus-arxiv-bridge/1.0 (mailto:noreply@local)'` per arXiv API guidelines (mailto in UA encouraged for soft rate-limit pool routing). Restored verbatim.

bridges_updated.name: openalex
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-A 'nexus-openalex-bridge/1.0 (mailto:noreply@local)'`; restored verbatim. Belt-and-braces with the mailto query parameter that already routes the call into OpenAlex polite-pool.

bridges_updated.name: lhc
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-A 'nexus-lhc-opendata-bridge/1.0'`; expanded slightly with anima-research URL for traceability.

bridges_updated.name: wikipedia
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-H 'accept: application/json'` (no UA). This cycle adds BOTH a polite UA (Wikipedia API ToS encourages identifying UA) AND restores the explicit Accept header (belt-and-braces; REST summary endpoint returns JSON regardless of Accept, but explicit is safer).

bridges_updated.name: uniprot
bridges_updated.rationale_for_change: pre-migration sent `-H 'accept: application/json'` (no UA). This cycle adds polite UA AND restores explicit Accept header. URL ends in `.json` so content negotiation already satisfied via extension; Accept header is belt-and-braces hygiene.

bridges_NOT_updated_explanation: these 8 never sent custom -A/-H pre-migration; they always used default curl UA and PASS unchanged. No env-var prefix added → behaviour-identical to ω-bridge-3.

validation.bash_3_2_compat_check: verified empty-array expansion via `${extra_args[@]+"${extra_args[@]}"}` idiom — required because macOS bash 3.2.57 + `set -u` would trap on bare `"${extra_args[@]}"` when array is empty (test reproduced and confirmed mitigation works)

header_send_confirmed_via_httpbin.method: BRIDGE_HELPER_VERBOSE=1 bash tool/bridge_helper.sh fetch <bridge> https://httpbin.org/headers 0 1 — httpbin echoes the headers it received in the response body

test_1_wikipedia_pattern.verbose_log: [bridge_helper] UA set: nexus-wikipedia-bridge/1.0 (+https://anima-research.local) / [bridge_helper] header set: accept: application/json
test_1_wikipedia_pattern.verdict: BOTH UA and Accept arrived at remote endpoint as set

test_2_arxiv_pattern.verdict: UA arrived at remote endpoint as set

test_3_backward_compat_no_env.verdict: default behaviour preserved when env vars unset — confirms backward-compat for the 8 untouched bridges

sha256_baseline_rotation.command: bash tool/bridge_sha256_rotate.sh --reason 'omega-bridge-4 header pass-through env-var injection (UA + accept restored)'
sha256_baseline_rotation.drift_correctly_detected: exactly the 8 bridges edited this cycle (cmb, nanograv, icecube, arxiv, openalex, lhc, wikipedia, uniprot); 0 false positives on the 8 untouched bridges

validation.regression_health_check_pre_rotation: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ FAIL total=16 pass=8 fail=8 tampered=8 duration_ms=3000 — expected: 8 sha256 mismatches from edits before rotation
validation.regression_health_check_post_rotation: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ PASS total=16 pass=16 fail=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=24000
validation.regression_health_check_final_after_cach: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ PASS total=16 pass=16 fail=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=31000
validation.verdict: no regression; all 8 modified bridges still PASS with the new env-prefixed helper invocation; all 8 untouched bridges unaffected

helper_sha_change_explanation: expected — 28 net LOC added (env-var doc block + extra_args assembly + applied to two curl call sites + bash-3.2 safe expansion idiom). All 16 already-migrated bridge .hexa files reference the helper by path, so the path is stable; only the helper body sha rotated.
constraints_honored: edited 8 bridge .hexa files in tool/ (cmb, nanograv, icecube, arxiv, openalex, lhc, wikipedia, uniprot — 10 call-site edits total)
NO live API hammering — used cached health for regression (live re-fetch only on first warm-up; subsequent calls cache-hit)
spot-tests against httpbin.org/headers (single benign call per pattern, 3 total) confirmed wire-level header injection
stopped on JSON written + spot-test confirmed headers send

rollback.helper_revert: git checkout HEAD -- tool/bridge_helper.sh   # restores ω-bridge-3 helper (sha 1f8c10bb)
rollback.after_rollback: bash tool/bridge_sha256_rotate.sh --reason 'rollback ω-bridge-4' to re-pin baseline (will rotate the same 8)
rollback.global_kill_switch: callers can simply omit the BRIDGE_HELPER_UA / BRIDGE_HELPER_HEADERS env vars in their cmd string — helper falls back to ω-bridge-3 behaviour identically (default curl UA, no extra -H). The env-var consumption is purely additive.

follow_ups_for_omega_bridge_5: still-pending from ω-bridge-2: fix uniprot F2 sentinel-bug (orthogonal to header work)
still-pending from ω-bridge-2: ETag/If-Modified-Since support in helper for upstreams that honor it
still-pending from ω-bridge-2: bridge_helper.sh stats subcommand (cache-hit-rate from .meta sidecar timestamps)
still-pending from ω-bridge-3: investigate why bridge_helper.sh was missing from working tree at ω-bridge-3 start; harden against drift

NEW (ω-bridge-4): consider promoting the env-export prefix into a tiny helper subcommand (e.g. `bridge_helper.sh fetch-as <bridge> --ua <ua> --header <h> -- <url> <ttl> <interval>`) — would let bridges drop the inline `BRIDGE_HELPER_UA='...' bash ...` boilerplate; cosmetic, not urgent

NEW (ω-bridge-4): per-call BRIDGE_HELPER_TIMEOUT is wired into helper but no bridge currently exercises it — could be used by horizons (NASA ephemeris is sometimes slow) to set a tighter user-facing budget while still benefiting from cache+retry
